briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: KVD 300 Deep runs 10-12ft — go-to for highland reservoirs and clear natural lakes in cold water. Fish off main lake points, bluff walls, bridge pilings, riprap. Parallel deeper edges. Twitch-twitch-pause with LONG pauses. Bright colors in shad kill conditions so fish see it from distance.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: KVD 300 Deep runs 10-12ft — go-to for highland reservoirs and clear natural lakes in cold water. Fish off main lake points, bluff walls, bridge pilings, riprap. Parallel deeper edges. Twitch-twit
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: A jerkbait is the best winter bait. Forward-facing sonar shows that long pauses are important not just because water is colder, but also to give bass time to come up from deeper water to meet
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, point, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...
Johnston: For smallmouth bass in cold water, they seem to key in on tube jigs or a swimbait better than jerkbaits. For largemouth, suspending jerkbaits and deep-drop jigs are the go-to.

Palaniuk: 'For winter jerkbaits, use a smaller 70-size reel with 7.1:1 gear ratio in cold water, then bump up to 8.1:1 as it warms into spring. The smaller spool means even a faster ratio isn't too fast.'

Hackney: 'For winter deep-water jig fishing on lowland reservoirs with stumps and channel swings in 6-8 feet, I use 16-20 pound fluorocarbon. On hard structure like rock I go 14-16 pound. Only downsize to 12 in extremely deep clear water'

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish follow but won't commit, extend your pause to 8-10 seconds and vary the cadence
